channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17697] lfs_migrate -A broken in 2.15 if source file uses PFL
item.description:
lfs_migrate -A seems to be broken if the file to be migrated uses a PFL layout: option -A is basically ignored and lfs migrate --copy is used, the file is migrated using the same PFL layout, and if the previous OST(s) in the old layout are disabled (eg. max_create_count=0), always the next available OST is picked up, leading to poor space balancing.

Example:

# lfs getstripe /oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5
/oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5
  lcm_layout_gen:    2
  lcm_mirror_count:  1
  lcm_entry_count:   2
    lcme_id:             1
    lcme_mirror_id:      0
    lcme_flags:          init
    lcme_extent.e_start: 0
    lcme_extent.e_end:   2199023255552
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: 69
      lmm_objects:
      - 0: { l_ost_idx: 69, l_fid: [0xc00000400:0x3dd08a:0x0] }

    lcme_id:             2
    lcme_mirror_id:      0
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 2199023255552
    lcme_extent.e_end:   EOF
      lmm_stripe_count:  8
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

# echo /oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5 | lfs_migrate -A -C 20 -D --non-block --no-rsync -y -v
/oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5: stripe_count=1,stripe_size=1048576 
lfs migrate -D --non-block --copy /oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5 "/oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-
done


# lfs getstripe /oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5
/oak/stanford/orgs/simes/jxding/jxding-desktop-Data-DQMC/8x8_tp-0.05_n1.0_thermal_perlmt_2/nflux1/n1.0/beta4.5_U6_mu-0.045/2024_01_04_113.h5
  lcm_layout_gen:    3
  lcm_mirror_count:  1
  lcm_entry_count:   2
    lcme_id:             1
    lcme_mirror_id:      0
    lcme_flags:          init
    lcme_extent.e_start: 0
    lcme_extent.e_end:   2199023255552
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: 193
      lmm_objects:
      - 0: { l_ost_idx: 193, l_fid: [0x3340000402:0x72d3ab:0x0] }

    lcme_id:             2
    lcme_mirror_id:      0
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 2199023255552
    lcme_extent.e_end:   EOF
      lmm_stripe_count:  8
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

193 is the next available OST index with max_create_count=2000. All migrated files are migrated to this OST which will fill up dramatically when doing a lot of migrations.

What we want is the previous behavior: lfs_migrate -A should restripe the file automatically to a plain layout as documented with OST space balancing controlled by the MDT. Was this change in 2.15 intended? I've seen a few tickets to support PFL, but while PFL is awesome, lfs_migrate -A is still be useful to migrate old PFL files to plain layout knowing the file size in advance and using stripe count = sqrt(filesize_in_GB) + 1 as documented in the man page.

In lfs_migrate, changing:

450 if ! $OPT_COMP && [ $\{layout_info[$l_comp_count]} -gt 0 ]; then
451 layout+="--copy $OLDNAME"
452 OPT_COMP=true
453 fi

to:

450 if ! $OPT_COMP && ! $OPT_AUTOSTRIPE && [ $\{layout_info[$l_comp_count]} -gt 0 ]; then
451 layout+="--copy $OLDNAME"
452 OPT_COMP=true
453 fi

seems to restore 2.12's behavior.

It's just an issue with the lfs_migrate script I believe, so no big deal, and let me know if I missed something obvious here.
item.summary: lfs_migrate -A broken in 2.15 if source file uses PFL

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: Thanks Andreas! I agree on the confusion between the two commands as the naming is similar, but I kind of like having lfs migrate as the base command, simple but robust, and another tool implementing heuristics like -A as a script so it can be easily adapted.

sthiell the separation of lfs_migrate vs. lfs migrate is confusing for many users, and there have been requests in the past to merge the "lfs_migrate" script functionality into the "lfs migrate" sub-command to avoid this confusion. I think the options "-A|--auto|--auto-stripe" and "-R|--restripe" from "lfs_migrate" could be moved over to "lfs migrate" relatively easily to start this process, rather than keeping them in the script.

Hi Andreas!

Thanks, that's an helpful pointer and I see that's a work in progress. If I backport the two following patches on 2.15.4, the PFL layout is still kept and less full OST(s) are properly selected when using lfs_migrate -A:

  • https://review.whamcloud.com/54600 "LU-16500 utils: 'lfs migrate' should select new OSTs"
  • https://review.whamcloud.com/49819 "LU-16500 utils: set default ost index for lfs migrate"

So it's much better as the next available OST won't fill up like before. Still, that doesn't match lfs_migrate -A's original behavior, which should be, according to the man page:

       -A     Automatically determine the stripe count for the file, using the algorithm count = sqrt(filesize_in_GB) + 1.  This option may not be specified at the same time as the -c or \-R options.

Perhaps there should be an additional option in `lfs_migrate` so that the sysadmin can choose to keep or not the PFL layout if -A is selected. In my case I would prefer not to keep the original PFL layout.
Stephane, can you please check if patch https://review.whamcloud.com/54600 "LU-16500 utils: 'lfs migrate' should select new OSTs" fixes this issue for you?
